page_content:
TLPL774 Urban Education (3 Credits) This seminar provides students with a broad overview of urban education as a ﬁeld of inquiry by examining the social context of urban schools, how transformative pedagogy is practiced and conceptualized as well as school reform. Credit Only Granted for: EDCI776 or TLPL774. Formerly: EDCI776.

TLPL775 Power, Privilege, Diversity and Teaching (3 Credits) This course critically examines the theoretic foundation of multicultural education with particular emphasis on the relationship between power, privilege, diversity and teaching. Credit Only Granted for: EDCI782 or TLPL775. Formerly: EDCI782.

TLPL776 Race and Whiteness in Education (3 Credits) This course asks: what is whiteness, white supremacy, and white privilege, and how do they function in U.S. educational systems? Students interrogate white onto-epistemologies and white settler-colonial logics; social constructions (and reconstructions) of race and whiteness; and institutional, structural, and systemic racism via research, teaching, policies, and practice. Credit Only Granted for: TLPL776 or TLPL788U. Formerly: TLPL788U.
